scenario: Team wants forms with company branding -- custom colors, fonts. Wonders styling capability.
question: Form custom CSS / theming?

options.text: 8 does not offer in-product Tasklist branding through Console, the Forms embeddable component supports CSS variable overrides for visual customization when embedded in custom applications. Organizations building custom task applications using the Camunda Forms JavaScript library can apply their own themes and brand colors through standard CSS customization mechanisms.

8 has NO documented in-product Tasklist branding feature (Pattern #43 -- empirically verified): no Console settings for logo upload, primary color pickers, or theme switching exist in the documented components/tasklist/ or components/console/ paths. Branding and visual customization require either embedding the Camunda Forms component in a custom application with CSS variable overrides, or building a completely custom task application that uses the Tasklist API as a backend while applying custom branding in the frontend layer.

While deep branding such as custom logos and color schemes does require embedding forms in a custom application, many branding needs can be addressed through CSS overrides in the embeddable Forms component. Full custom Tasklist development is the most flexible approach but also highest effort. Organizations should evaluate whether CSS-level customization meets requirements before committing to full custom development.
